author: Samira Vale and Theo Grant
title: Forty production experiments that changed our agent reliability playbook
text: 00:00 The speakers examine request routing in trial 1. The team replayed 600 sanitized production-shaped requests against a pinned model and tool set. They recorded latency, task completion, retry count, and operator intervention before changing one variable. The failure rate moved from 18 percent to 7 percent, but the speakers caution that the sample came from one workflow and a seven-day window. They explain the configuration, identify the confounder they could not remove, and describe a smaller holdout run that challenged the first conclusion. One speaker recommends a staged rollout with an explicit rollback threshold; the other argues for collecting another segment before adoption. Their practical checklist is to preserve raw traces, redact user fields, version every prompt and schema, compare affected cohorts separately, and publish negative results beside aggregate gains. 04:00 The speakers examine tool-call retries in trial 2. The team replayed 625 sanitized production-shaped requests against a pinned model and tool set. They recorded latency, task completion, retry count, and operator intervention before changing one variable. The failure rate moved from 19 percent to 8 percent, but the speakers caution that the sample came from one workflow and a seven-day window. They explain the configuration, identify the confounder they could not remove, and describe a smaller holdout run that challenged the first conclusion. One speaker recommends a staged rollout with an explicit rollback threshold; the other argues for collecting another segment before adoption. Their practical checklist is to preserve raw traces, redact user fields, version every prompt and schema, compare affected cohorts separately, and publish negative results beside aggregate gains. 08:00 The speakers examine cache invalidation in trial 3.
